title: Shani Chalisa
intro: Shri Shani Chalisa - Hymn to Lord Shani
description:
॥ Shri Shani Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥

Jai Ganesh girija suvan,
Mangal karan kripal.
Deenan ke dukh door kari,
Keejai nath nihal.
Jai jai shri shanidev prabhu,
Sunahu vinay maharaj.
Karahu kripa he ravi tanay,
Rakhabu jan ki laaj.

॥ Chaupai ॥

Jayati jayati shanidev dayala.
Karat sada bhaktan pratipala.
Chari bhuja, tanu shyam virajai.
Mathe ratan mukut chhabi chhajai.
Param vishal manohar bhala.
Tedhi drishti bhrukuti vikarala.
Kundal shravan chamacham chamake.
Hiye maal muktan mani damkai.
Kar mein gada trishul kuthara.
Pal bich karain arihin samhara.
Pingal, krishno, chhaya, nandan.
Yama, konastha, raudra, dukh bhanjan.
Souri, mand shani dash nama.
Bhanu putra pujahin sab kama.
Japar prabhu prasanna havain jahin.
Rankahun raav karain kshan mahin.
Parvatahu trun hoi niharat.
Trunahu ko parvat kari darat.
Raj milat van ramahi deenhayo.
Kaikeihun ki mati hari leenhayo.
Vanahun mein mrug kapat dikhai.
Matu janaki gayi churai.
Lashanahin shakti vikal karidara.
Machiga dal mein hahakara.
Ravan ki gati-mati bourai.
Ramchandra son bair badhai.
Diyo keet kari kanchan lanka.
Baji bajrang veer ki danka.
Nrup vikram par tuhi pagu dhara.
Chitra mayur nigali gai hara.
Har naulakha lagyo chori.
Haath pair darvayo tori.
Bhari dasha nikrusht dikhayo.
Telahin ghar kolhu chalvayo.
Vinay rag deepak mahain keenhayon.
Tab prasanna prabhu havai sukh deenhayon.
Harishchandra nrup nari bikani.
Aaphun bhare dom ghar pani.
Taise nal par dasha sirani.
Bhunji-meen kud gayi pani.
Shri shankarahin gahyo jab jai.
Parvati ko sati karai.
Tanik vikalokat hi kari risa.
Nabh udi gato gaurisut sisa.
Pandav par bhai dasha tumhari.
Bachi dropadi hoti udhari.
Kaurav ke bhi gati mati marayo.
Yuddh mahabharat kari darayo.
Ravi kaham mukh maham dhari tatkala.
Le-kar kudi paryo patala.
Shesh dev-lakhi vinati lai.
Ravi ko mukh te diyo chhudai.
Vahan prabhu ke saat sujana.
Jag diggaj gardabh mrug svana.
Jambuk singh aadi nakh dhari.
So phal jyotish kahat pukari.
Gaj vahan lakshmi gruh aavain.
Hay te sukh sampatti upjavai.
Gardabh hani karai bahu kaja.
Singh siddhkar raj samaja.
Jambuk buddhi nasht kar darai.
Mrug de kasht pran samharai.
Jab aavahin svan savari.
Chori aadi hoy dar bhari.
Taisahi chari charan yeh nama.
Swarn lauh chandi aru tama.
Lauh charan par jab prabhu aavain.
Dhan jan sampatti nasht karavain.
Samata tamr rajat shubhkari.
Swarn sarvasukh mangal bhari.
Jo yeh shani charitra nit gavai.
Kabahun na dasha nikrusht satavai.
Adbhut nath dikhavain leela.
Karain shatru ke nashi bali dheela.
Jo pandit suyogya bulvai.
Vidhivat shani grah shanti karai.
Peepal jal shani divas chadhavat.
Deep daan dai bahu sukh pavat.
Kahat ram sundar prabhu dasa.
Shani sumirat sukh hot prakasha.

॥ Doha ॥

Path shanishchar dev ko,
Ki hon 'bhakt' taiyar.
Karat path chalis din,
Ho bhavsagar paar.
meaning:
॥ Shri Shani Chalisa ॥

॥ Doha ॥

Jai Ganesh girija suvan,
Victory to Ganesha, son of Girija,
Mangal karan kripal.
You are the auspicious and compassionate one.
Deenan ke dukh door kari,
Remove the sorrows of the poor,
Keejai nath nihal.
O Lord, make us blessed.
Jai jai shri shanidev prabhu,
Victory, victory to Lord Shani Dev,
Sunahu vinay maharaj.
Listen to my prayer, O King.
Karahu kripa he ravi tanay,
Show mercy, O Son of the Sun,
Rakhabu jan ki laaj.
Protect the honor of your devotee.

॥ Chaupai ॥

Jayati jayati shanidev dayala.
Victory to the compassionate Shani Dev,
Karat sada bhaktan pratipala.
Who always protects his devotees.
Chari bhuja, tanu shyam virajai.
You have four arms and a dark-complexioned body,
Mathe ratan mukut chhabi chhajai.
A jewel-studded crown shines on your head.
Param vishal manohar bhala.
Your forehead is vast and beautiful,
Tedhi drishti bhrukuti vikarala.
Your gaze and fierce eyebrows are formidable.
Kundal shravan chamacham chamake.
Earrings sparkle in your ears,
Hiye maal muktan mani damkai.
And a pearl necklace glitters on your chest.
Kar mein gada trishul kuthara.
You hold a mace, trident, and axe in your hands,
Pal bich karain arihin samhara.
Destroying enemies in a moment.
Pingal, krishno, chhaya, nandan.
You are Pingal, Krishno, and Chhaya Nandan,
Yama, konastha, raudra, dukh bhanjan.
Yama, Konastha, Raudra, and the destroyer of sorrows.
Souri, mand shani dash nama.
Souri, Manda, and Shani are your ten names,
Bhanu putra pujahin sab kama.
O Son of the Sun, worshipping you fulfills all desires.
Japar prabhu prasanna havain jahin.
Those on whom you are pleased,
Rankahun raav karain kshan mahin.
You turn a pauper into a king in an instant.
Parvatahu trun hoi niharat.
You see a mountain as a blade of grass,
Trunahu ko parvat kari darat.
And turn a blade of grass into a mountain.
Raj milat van ramahi deenhayo.
You gave Ram the forest instead of a kingdom,
Kaikeihun ki mati hari leenhayo.
And swayed the intellect of Kaikeyi.
Vanahun mein mrug kapat dikhai.
By showing a deceitful deer in the forest,
Matu janaki gayi churai.
Mother Janaki was stolen away.
Lashanahin shakti vikal karidara.
You struck Laxman with the Shakti weapon,
Machiga dal mein hahakara.
Causing chaos in the army.
Ravan ki gati-mati bourai.
Ravan's intellect was corrupted,
Ramchandra son bair badhai.
And he provoked enmity with Ramchandra.
Diyo keet kari kanchan lanka.
You turned the golden Lanka into dust,
Baji bajrang veer ki danka.
And the glory of Bajrang Bali was proclaimed.
Nrup vikram par tuhi pagu dhara.
When you stepped on King Vikramaditya,
Chitra mayur nigali gai hara.
The picture of the peacock swallowed the necklace.
Har naulakha lagyo chori.
He was accused of stealing the nine-gem necklace,
Haath pair darvayo tori.
And had his hands and feet broken.
Bhari dasha nikrusht dikhayo.
You showed him a difficult phase,
Telahin ghar kolhu chalvayo.
And made him work the oil press.
Vinay rag deepak mahain keenhayon.
He sang Raga Deepak in prayer,
Tab prasanna prabhu havai sukh deenhayon.
And then you were pleased and gave him happiness.
Harishchandra nrup nari bikani.
King Harishchandra had to sell his wife,
Aaphun bhare dom ghar pani.
And he himself drew water at the Dom's house.
Taise nal par dasha sirani.
Similarly, King Nala faced your hardship,
Bhunji-meen kud gayi pani.
When his fried fish jumped back into the water.
Shri shankarahin gahyo jab jai.
When you affected Lord Shankar,
Parvati ko sati karai.
You caused Parvati to become Sati.
Tanik vikalokat hi kari risa.
By your mere glance of anger,
Nabh udi gato gaurisut sisa.
The head of Gauri’s son flew into the sky.
Pandav par bhai dasha tumhari.
You brought hardship upon the Pandavas,
Bachi dropadi hoti udhari.
But Draupadi was saved by your grace.
Kaurav ke bhi gati mati marayo.
You destroyed the intellect of the Kauravas,
Yuddh mahabharat kari darayo.
Leading to the Mahabharata war.
Ravi kaham mukh maham dhari tatkala.
You instantly put the Sun in your mouth,
Le-kar kudi paryo patala.
And jumped into the netherworld.
Shesh dev-lakhi vinati lai.
The gods witnessed this and prayed to you,
Ravi ko mukh te diyo chhudai.
And you released the Sun from your mouth.
Vahan prabhu ke saat sujana.
You have seven clever mounts,
Jag diggaj gardabh mrug svana.
Elephant, Horse, Donkey, Deer, and Dog.
Jambuk singh aadi nakh dhari.
Jackal and Lion are also among them,
So phal jyotish kahat pukari.
The fruit of which astrology loudly declares.
Gaj vahan lakshmi gruh aavain.
Elephant mount brings Lakshmi to the home,
Hay te sukh sampatti upjavai.
The horse mount brings happiness and wealth.
Gardabh hani karai bahu kaja.
The donkey mount causes loss in work,
Singh siddhkar raj samaja.
The lion mount grants royal success.
Jambuk buddhi nasht kar darai.
The jackal destroys intellect,
Mrug de kasht pran samharai.
The deer causes pain and suffering.
Jab aavahin svan savari.
When you arrive on the dog mount,
Chori aadi hoy dar bhari.
There is great fear of theft.
Taisahi chari charan yeh nama.
Similarly, you have four foundations,
Swarn lauh chandi aru tama.
Gold, Iron, Silver, and Copper.
Lauh charan par jab prabhu aavain.
When you come on iron feet,
Dhan jan sampatti nasht karavain.
You destroy wealth, people, and property.
Samata tamr rajat shubhkari.
Copper and Silver provide balance,
Swarn sarvasukh mangal bhari.
Gold brings all happiness and prosperity.
Jo yeh shani charitra nit gavai.
Whoever recites this Shani Charitra daily,
Kabahun na dasha nikrusht satavai.
A difficult phase never torments them.
Adbhut nath dikhavain leela.
The Lord shows amazing miracles,
Karain shatru ke nashi bali dheela.
And destroys the strength of enemies.
Jo pandit suyogya bulvai.
By calling a qualified pundit,
Vidhivat shani grah shanti karai.
And performing Shani Shanti rituals.
Peepal jal shani divas chadhavat.
Offering water to the Peepal tree on Saturdays,
Deep daan dai bahu sukh pavat.
And donating lamps brings great happiness.
Kahat ram sundar prabhu dasa.
Says Ram Sundar, servant of the Lord,
Shani sumirat sukh hot prakasha.
Remembering Shani brings light and happiness.

॥ Doha ॥

Path shanishchar dev ko,
The devotee prepares to recite the Chalisa,
Ki hon 'bhakt' taiyar.
Of Lord Shanishchar Dev.
Karat path chalis din,
By reciting it for forty days,
Ho bhavsagar paar.
One crosses the ocean of existence.

faq.a1: The Shani Chalisa is a sacred devotional hymn consisting of 40 verses dedicated to Shani Dev (the deity representing the planet Saturn). In Hindu astrology and philosophy, Shani Dev is regarded as the "God of Justice" and the "Giver of Fruits of Karma." The Chalisa serves as a prayer to seek his blessings, guidance, and the strength to live a life of discipline and righteousness.
faq.q2: Is there a "best" time to recite the Shani Chalisa?
faq.a2: While you can recite the Shani Chalisa at any time, Saturdays are considered the most auspicious day as they are dedicated to Shani Dev. In terms of timing, reciting it during the evening hours (after sunset) is often considered highly effective. However, if you wish to recite it daily, consistency is more important than specific timing.
faq.q3: Is it true that we should fear Shani Dev?
faq.a3: This is a common misconception. Shani Dev is not a deity to be feared; rather, he is a divine judge. He rewards or corrects individuals based solely on their Karma (actions). He is only stern with those who walk the path of dishonesty or injustice. Reciting the Shani Chalisa is actually a way to calm the mind, remove fear, and gain the courage to face life’s challenges with integrity.
faq.q4: What are the main benefits of reciting the Shani Chalisa?
faq.a4: Devotees recite the Shani Chalisa for several spiritual and personal benefits: Navigating Difficult Phases: It is widely believed to provide mental strength and peace during challenging astrological periods like Sade Sati or Dhaiya. Encouraging Discipline: As Shani Dev represents discipline, reciting this hymn helps cultivate focus, patience, and seriousness in one's life. Removing Obstacles: It helps clear hurdles in personal and professional growth. Spiritual Protection: It serves as a shield against negative thoughts and influences.
faq.q5: Are there strict rituals or rules to follow?
faq.a5: Shani Dev appreciates simplicity and truthfulness over elaborate rituals. While you should maintain basic hygiene and sit in a clean, quiet space, there are no complicated rules. It is traditional to light a lamp with mustard oil (sarson ka tel) while reciting, but the most important "rule" is to have a pure heart and avoid dishonesty while seeking his blessings.
faq.q6: Who can recite the Shani Chalisa?
faq.a6: Anyone can recite the Shani Chalisa. It is a universal prayer. You do not need any formal initiation or a Guru to begin. Whether you are facing a tough time, looking to improve your self-discipline, or simply seeking divine justice and balance in your life, you can recite it with full faith and devotion.
faq.q7: Can I recite it on days other than Saturday?
faq.a7: Yes, absolutely. While Saturday holds special significance, the Divine hears your prayers on every day of the week. If you are going through a difficult situation or need mental clarity, you can recite the Shani Chalisa whenever you feel the need. Your sincerity is what matters most to the deity.
